In [2]:
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
%matplotlib inline
In [3]:
mpg_df=pd.read_csv("car-mpg.csv")

In [17]:
X=mpg_df.drop('mpg', axis=1)
In [18]:
y=mpg_df.pop('mpg')

In [22]:
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
In [25]:
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test =train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.30, random_state=1)

from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
In [35]:

regression_model.fit(X_train,y_train)

Create a Model using Statsmodel¶

In [59]:
import statsmodels.formula.api as smf
In [62]:
data_train = pd.concat([X_train, y_train], axis=1)
In [64]:
data_train.head()
Out[64]:
cyl disp hp wt acc yr origin car_type mpg
350 4 105.0 63 2215 14.9 81 1 1 34.7
59 4 97.0 54 2254 23.5 72 2 1 23.0
120 4 121.0 112 2868 15.5 73 2 1 19.0
12 8 400.0 150 3761 9.5 70 1 0 15.0
349 4 91.0 68 1985 16.0 81 3 1 34.1
In [66]:
regression_model2= smf.ols(formula='mpg~ cyl + disp + hp + wt + acc + yr + origin + car_type', data= data_train).fit()
In [67]:
regression_model2.params
Out[67]:

car_type      6.061247
dtype: float64

Slight presence of Heteroscaditicity¶

In [ ]:
